error_signature: Bad request - please check your parameters
title: Invalid DateTime format in HTTP node for Outlook meetings
symptom: When attempting to create a meeting in Outlook using the HTTP node, an error occurs stating 'Bad request - please check your parameters' with an invalid DateTime message.
applies_when: Using HTTP node to create Outlook meetings
DateTime values are in ISO format
not_this_if: DateTime values are not in ISO format
root_cause: The HTTP node requires DateTime fields to be set as expressions rather than fixed values. If the expressions are not properly configured, the node will send the literal string instead of the parsed DateTime, leading to a bad request error.
fix_steps: Ensure the DateTime fields are set to 'Expressions' instead of 'Fixed'
If issues persist, delete the HTTP node and create a new one
Re-enter the JSON body with the correct expressions
agent_instruction: Set the DateTime fields in the HTTP node to use expressions and recreate the node if necessary.
